sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Wheaton Precious Metals Recognized by Corporate Knights as one of the 2026 Global 100?Most Sustainable Corporations
feed.summary: Wheaton Precious Metals has been named to Corporate Knights' 2026 Global 100 Most Sustainable Corporations list for the third consecutive year, recognizing its leadership in sustainable value creation. The company's CEO, Randy Smallwood, emphasized that sustainability is fundamental to their long-term value creation and reflects the quality of their mining partners and the importance of mining to the clean economy. Wheaton also highlights its commitment to climate solutions through initiatives like the Future of Mining Challenge, which aims to advance efficiency and environmental innovation in the sector.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Discusses Honeywell (HON)'s Spinoffs
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Honeywell International Inc.'s (HON) strategic spinoffs, which are also a key focus for analysts like JPMorgan and Mizuho. JPMorgan upgraded HON to Overweight, citing potential opportunities despite short-term earnings opacity due to the splits, while Mizuho maintained an Outperform rating. Cramer specifically mentioned the upcoming quantum computing spinoff as particularly exciting.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (AUB) Atlantic Union Bankshares Corporation Posts Q4 Adjusted EPS $0.97 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $0.86
feed.summary: Atlantic Union Bankshares Corporation (AUB) reported adjusted earnings per share of $0.97 for the fourth quarter, surpassing FactSet's estimate of $0.86. The company's Q4 revenue also exceeded analyst expectations, reaching $387.2 million against an estimate of $379.0 million. This positive earnings flash indicates strong performance for the financial institution.

feed.title: Raymond James bolsters US advisory ranks with two strategic hires
feed.summary: Raymond James has expanded its U.S. advisory team by adding two experienced wealth professionals to its independent and employee-advisor channels. One advisor joined Raymond James Financial Services in South Carolina, bringing approximately $247 million in client assets, while Michael Thomason and senior registered client service associate Donna Chepenik moved to Raymond James & Associates in Texas with over $100 million in client assets. These strategic hires underscore the firm's advisor-recruiting strategy and its appeal to established practices seeking robust platform support and growth resources.

feed.title: Don't Ever Count Out Microsoft (MSFT) CEO, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Microsoft (MSFT) CEO Satya Nadella's strong leadership amidst AI spending uncertainties, advising against counting him out. Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley maintained "Buy" and "Overweight" ratings respectively, with price targets of $655 and $650, despite concerns about AI data center power costs impacting free cash flow. The article also touches on potential increases in software spending for 2026.

feed.title: Apple Spent Years Downplaying AI Chatbots. Now Siri Is Becoming One
feed.summary: Apple, which previously downplayed the utility of AI chatbots, is reportedly transforming its Siri assistant into a full-fledged, system-wide AI chatbot. This shift, according to Bloomberg, indicates Apple's concession that chatbots are a preferred interface for AI, moving away from its previous philosophy of subtly embedding AI within device features. The new Siri, code-named Campos, will be deeply integrated across Apple's operating systems and offer an experience more akin to ChatGPT.

feed.title: Vest Financial LLC Purchases 22,004 Shares of Texas Instruments Incorporated $TXN
feed.summary: Vest Financial LLC increased its stake in Texas Instruments Incorporated (NASDAQ:TXN) by 42.4% in the third quarter, purchasing an additional 22,004 shares and bringing its total holdings to 73,920 shares valued at $13.58 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in the semiconductor company. Analyst ratings for TXN are mixed, with a consensus "Hold" rating and a target price of $194.22.

feed.title: Less Than 20 Companies Will Scale Humanoid Robots for Manufacturing by 2028: Gartner
feed.summary: Gartner predicts that by 2028, fewer than 20 companies will successfully implement humanoid robots for manufacturing and supply chain use cases in production environments, despite their potential. The technology is considered immature with significant barriers like high costs and integration complexity, making it suitable mainly for companies with a high-risk appetite. Polyfunctional robots are expected to remain the superior solution for most companies.

feed.title: TIM S.A. Signs Long-Term Tower Deal with American Tower to Streamline Network Infrastructure
feed.summary: TIM S.A. has approved a long-term agreement with American Tower do Brasil, consolidating contracts for approximately 9,000 towers through 2034. This strategic move aims to streamline governance, improve cost predictability, and support the sustainable expansion of TIM's mobile network as part of its Lease Efficiency Plan. The deal is expected to enhance operational efficiency and reinforce TIM's partnership with American Tower.

feed.title: Southern Missouri Bancorp Posts Strong Q2 Results, New Buyback
feed.summary: Southern Missouri Bancorp (SMBC) reported a strong Q2 2026, with net income up 23.9% year-on-year to $18.2 million and diluted EPS of $1.62. The bank also declared its 127th consecutive quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share and authorized a new share repurchase program for up to 550,000 shares, signaling confidence in its financial strength. Analysts currently rate SMBC stock as a Buy with a $70.00 price target, and TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, also provides an "Outperform" signal due to strong financial and technical indicators.

feed.title: Assessing Sun Communities (SUI) Valuation After Recent Flat Multi Year Share Performance
feed.summary: This article assesses the valuation of Sun Communities (SUI) whose shares have been flat over the past three years. Despite a recent slight decline, the stock shows modest momentum, trading at a 4.8x Price-to-Sales multiple, which is lower than peers and the industry average, suggesting it might be undervalued. A Discounted Cash Flow model further supports an undervaluation, estimating a fair value significantly higher than the current share price.

feed.title: Brown & Brown Launches Fully Integrated National Healthcare Practice
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE: BRO) has announced the launch of Brown & Brown Healthcare, a new fully integrated national practice designed to provide comprehensive risk and insurance solutions for healthcare organizations. The practice combines over 140 professionals, co-led by Matthew Siciliani and Tracy Hoffman with strategic guidance from chairman Bob Dubraski, to offer specialized services across various healthcare risk areas. This initiative aims to address the complex and evolving risks faced by the healthcare sector, leveraging the company's extensive reach and expertise.

feed.title: News Corp Details Expanded $2 Billion Share Buyback Plan
feed.summary: News Corp has expanded its share repurchase program to a total of $2 billion, targeting Nasdaq-listed Class A and Class B common shares. This capital management initiative, facilitated by Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, aims to enhance shareholder value and will be influenced by market conditions and stock price. The buyback specifically excludes ASX-listed CDIs and does not require shareholder approval.

feed.title: Warren Buffett Was Never Just a Value Investor. Here’s the Real Secret to His Investing Success
feed.summary: A new research paper by Kai Wu reveals that Warren Buffett's investment success was not primarily driven by traditional value investing but by consistently buying high-quality businesses with strong intangible assets. His outperformance can largely be explained by factor exposures to intangible value and quality, rather than non-replicable stock-picking skill. The article suggests that individual investors can replicate Buffett's returns by systematically investing in companies with high intangible value and quality, a strategy applicable across various markets.

feed.title: Henry Schein to Webcast Fourth Quarter 2025 Conference Call on Tuesday, February 24, 2026, at 8:00 A.M. ET
feed.summary: Henry Schein, Inc. (Nasdaq: HSIC) announced it will release its fourth quarter 2025 financial results on Tuesday, February 24, 2026, before the stock market opens. The company will host a live webcast of its earnings conference call on the same day at 8:00 a.m. ET, featuring Chairman and CEO Stanley M. Bergman and CFO Ronald N. South. Interested parties can access the webcast and a replay on the Henry Schein investor relations website.

feed.title: Atlantic Union Bankshares Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year Financial Results
feed.summary: Atlantic Union Bankshares Corporation reported net income of $109.0 million for the fourth quarter of 2025 and $261.8 million for the full year 2025. The company showed strong performance attributed to disciplined execution and the successful integration of the Sandy Spring Bancorp, Inc. acquisition, despite merger-related charges. Key financial highlights include increased net interest income, improved asset quality with decreased nonperforming assets, and an increase in total assets to $37.6 billion.

feed.title: 140 specialists join Brown & Brown to tackle complex healthcare risks
feed.summary: Brown & Brown (NYSE: BRO) has launched a new integrated National Healthcare Practice, Brown & Brown Healthcare, bringing together over 140 professionals to address complex risks in the healthcare sector. Co-led by Matthew Siciliani and Tracy Hoffman with Bob Dubraski as chairman, the practice will offer end-to-end services, including professional liability, managed care reinsurance, and workers' compensation, aiming to provide stronger market access and competitive programs for healthcare organizations globally. The initiative is designed to deliver comprehensive risk management solutions and enhance support for hundreds of customers, placing billions in premiums domestically and internationally.

feed.title: BD and Ypsomed develop new version of Neopak XtraFlow syringe
feed.summary: BD and Ypsomed are expanding their partnership to develop a 5.5ml version of the BD Neopak XtraFlow glass prefillable syringe, compatible with Ypsomed's YpsoMate 5.5 autoinjector platform. This development aims to offer more options for pharmaceutical companies and patients needing high-volume subcutaneous self-injections, especially for conditions requiring larger doses. The new syringe incorporates advanced BD technologies, including thinner cannulas and a shorter needle, to improve drug delivery efficiency for high-volume and high-viscosity medications.

feed.title: 1 Reason to Sell AMAT and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
feed.summary: This article suggests selling Applied Materials (AMAT) despite its recent stock jump, citing concerns about its projected revenue growth of only 2.4% over the next 12 months, which is significantly lower than its past five-year annual growth. The author argues that the stock's current valuation at 33x forward P/E, while fair, offers limited upside compared to potential downside risks. Instead, the article recommends looking into high-quality industrials businesses benefiting from upgrade cycles or other "High Quality stocks" featured in their curated lists.

feed.title: NextEra Energy, Inc.'s (NYSE:NEE) Popularity With Investors Is Under Threat From Overpricing
feed.summary: NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) is trading at a P/E ratio of 26.9x, which is higher than nearly half of other companies in the US market. Despite this, the company experienced a 6.7% decline in profits over the last year, although EPS has grown 60% over three years. Analysts forecast a 14% annual growth for NextEra Energy over the next three years, aligning with the broader market's 12% growth forecast, suggesting its high P/E might not be justified.

feed.title: Verdence Capital Advisors LLC Makes New Investment in W.W. Grainger, Inc. $GWW
feed.summary: Verdence Capital Advisors LLC has made a new investment of 1,007 shares, valued at approximately $985,000, in W.W. Grainger, Inc. (GWW) during the third quarter. This move comes as institutional investors now own about 80.7% of the company, following adjustments by several other hedge funds. Despite positive Q3 earnings that beat analyst estimates and a declared quarterly dividend, insiders sold a significant number of shares totaling over $26 million in the last quarter.

feed.title: Don't Ignore The Insider Selling In Goldman Sachs Group
feed.summary: A recent insider sale at Goldman Sachs Group saw Lead Independent Director David Viniar sell US$66m worth of shares, though this represented only 8.6% of his holding. While the sale occurred at the current stock price, which is less concerning than a sale at a lower price, the overall insider transaction trend for the past year shows more selling than buying. Despite this, insiders still own a significant 0.5% of the company, valued at US$1.4b, and the company is reporting growing earnings.

feed.title: WEX Inc. $WEX Shares Sold by Verdence Capital Advisors LLC
feed.summary: Verdence Capital Advisors LLC significantly reduced its stake in WEX Inc. (NYSE:WEX) by 89% in the third quarter of 2025, selling 31,532 shares and retaining 3,910 shares. This move contrasts with other major institutions like Boston Partners, Norges Bank, and Goldman Sachs, which either initiated or substantially increased their positions in WEX. Despite Verdence's sell-off, institutional investors collectively own about 97.47% of the company’s stock, and WEX continues to receive a "Hold" consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $171.36.

feed.title: NorthCrest Asset Manangement LLC Has $652,000 Holdings in Motorola Solutions, Inc. $MSI
feed.summary: NorthCrest Asset Management LLC significantly reduced its stake in Motorola Solutions (NYSE:MSI) by 87.7% in Q3, now holding 1,423 shares valued at $652,000. Despite this, Motorola Solutions reported strong Q3 earnings, beating estimates with $4.06 EPS and $3.01 billion in revenue, and increased its quarterly dividend. Institutional investors collectively own 84.17% of the stock, with analysts generally rating it a "Moderate Buy" and a consensus price target around $491.86.

feed.title: Verdence Capital Advisors LLC Makes New $1.16 Million Investment in The Kroger Co. $KR
feed.summary: Verdence Capital Advisors LLC has made a new $1.16 million investment in The Kroger Co. (NYSE:KR) by purchasing 17,075 shares during the third quarter. Institutional investors now hold approximately 80.93% of Kroger's stock. The company recently reported Q3 EPS of $1.05 and set FY2025 guidance at $4.75-$4.80 EPS, while analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average price target of $73.80.

feed.title: Musinsa emerges as dark horse in race for Hoka distribution rights
feed.summary: Musinsa, a leading Korean fashion platform, has entered the competition for the domestic distribution rights of the popular running shoe brand Hoka. This move positions Musinsa as a strong contender against larger fashion conglomerates, leveraging its marketing and branding expertise. The battle for Hoka's distribution highlights the brand's rapid growth in Korea and its appeal as a premium sports brand in a cooling luxury market.

feed.title: 3 Top Growth Stocks With Significant Insider Ownership
feed.summary: This article identifies three growth stocks—AAON, Estée Lauder Companies, and On Holding—with significant insider ownership, suggesting strong confidence from company insiders despite recent market volatility. It details each company's financial performance, growth forecasts, and strategic initiatives, while also noting that some of these stocks might be overvalued according to Simply Wall St's valuation reports. The piece emphasizes the importance of insider ownership as an indicator of a company's future prospects and alignment with shareholder interests.

feed.title: What Catalysts Could Shift The Story Behind Cummins (CMI) Now?
feed.summary: The article discusses how a recent change in Cummins' (CMI) price target has reignited investor interest, prompting a re-evaluation of the company's prospects by analysts. It emphasizes that while analyst price targets are important reference points, they are only one factor in assessing a stock's true value. The article encourages investors to use the Simply Wall St Community and its "Narratives" feature to understand the deeper story behind the numbers and how new information could influence the stock's fair value.

feed.title: XP Inc. (XP) Valuation Check After Jefferies Buy Rating And Q3 Beat
feed.summary: XP Inc. (XP) has garnered attention with a new "Buy" rating from Jefferies and a strong Q3 performance, leading to plans for a cash dividend and share repurchase program. Despite recent stock momentum, the company appears undervalued with a P/E of 10.4x, significantly lower than its industry peers and its estimated fair P/E. Both P/E and DCF models suggest an intrinsic discount, prompting investors to consider if the market is accurately pricing growth potential.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Stock Jumps 8% As Trump’s Davos Remarks Calm Markets
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock surged 8% due to positive market reactions to President Trump's calming remarks at Davos, significant AI infrastructure wins, and fresh analyst upgrades. Investors are increasingly confident in AMD's competitive stance against rivals like Nvidia and Intel, propelled by strong data center growth, new AI-enabled PC product launches, and key partnerships with companies like OpenAI and Oracle. This reflects a growing belief in AMD's long-term earning potential, with attention now turning to Q4 2025 earnings.

feed.title: Earnings Scheduled For January 22, 2026
feed.summary: This article lists companies scheduled to report earnings on January 22, 2026. It categorizes reporting companies into "Before The Bell" and "After The Bell" and provides anticipated earnings per share and revenue figures for each. Key companies mentioned include Abbott Laboratories, Alcoa, Intel, and Procter & Gamble.

feed.title: SPY ETF Daily Update, 1/22/2026
feed.summary: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) is up 0.59% in pre-market trading due to easing geopolitical worries and anticipation of the upcoming personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index report. SPY, which tracks the S&P 500 Index, has a Moderate Buy rating from TipRanks' ETF analyst consensus, with an implied upside potential of 17.03% based on an average price target of $802.14. Key catalysts for SPY include major U.S. economic data releases and corporate earnings reports.

feed.title: Dominion Energy to host vegetation management workshop in Charleston
feed.summary: Dominion Energy is holding a vegetation management workshop in Charleston to inform residents about upcoming tree trimming to protect its overhead power lines. This initiative is part of the utility's five-year cycle to enhance safety and reliability, as trees are a primary cause of power outages. The workshop will feature maps of affected neighborhoods, and property owners will receive notification two weeks before trimming begins.

feed.title: trivago N.V.'s Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings Release Scheduled for February 3, 2026; Webcast Scheduled for February 4, 2026
feed.summary: trivago N.V. (NASDAQ: TRVG) announced its fourth-quarter 2025 financial results will be released on Tuesday, February 3, 2026, after market close. Following the release, management will host a webcast on Wednesday, February 4, 2026, at 2:15 PM CET / 8:15 AM EST. Details and access to the webcast replay will be available on the company's Investor Relations website.

feed.title: United States Aroma Ingredients Market to Reach US$ 1.8 Billion
feed.summary: The global aroma ingredients market is projected to grow from US$ 4.4 billion in 2022 to US$ 6.1 billion by 2031, with a CAGR of 4.3%. The market's expansion is driven by the flavor, fragrance, and personal care industries adopting innovative, natural, and functional solutions, with a strong demand for premium flavors and fragrances. Key players like BASF SE, International Flavors & Fragrances Inc., and Robertet SA are driving innovation and supply in this market, which is segmented by application (food & beverages, personal care & cosmetics, pharmaceuticals) and type (natural and synthetic aromas).

feed.title: ADM's Premix Portfolio
feed.summary: ADM offers custom blending services and a range of specialty ingredients for livestock producers and pet food manufacturers in North America. Their premix portfolio includes tailor-made vitamin, mineral, and dry ingredient blends for both livestock and pet nutrition. ADM leverages its extensive research, manufacturing, and logistics capabilities to provide solution-based ingredients and quality products.

feed.title: Bear of the Day: Lamb Weston (LW)
feed.summary: Lamb Weston (LW), a global manufacturer of frozen potato products, has been designated a Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell) due to analysts' bearish outlook on its earnings. Despite exceeding consensus expectations in its latest quarter, the company's shares plunged, with sales growth remaining weak. The article highlights the importance of focusing on stocks with a Zacks Rank #1 or #2 for stronger earnings outlook and potential gains.

feed.title: United States Gut and Digestive Health Ingredients Market to hit US$ 6.93 Billion by 2031 | Major Companies 2026 - Chr. Hansen Holding A/S, DuPont de Nemours, Inc., Nestlé S.A.
feed.summary: The global Gut and Digestive Health Ingredients Market, valued at US$ 12.4 billion in 2022, is projected to reach US$ 23.1 billion by 2031, growing at an 8.1% CAGR. This growth is driven by innovations in probiotics, prebiotics, and enzymes, alongside increasing consumer awareness of digestive wellness and preventive nutrition. Key players like Chr. Hansen Holding A/S, DuPont de Nemours, Inc., and Nestlé S.A. are leading the market with science-backed ingredients used in functional foods, dietary supplements, and clinical nutrition products.

feed.title: Arctic Wolf detects surge in automated Fortinet FortiGate Firewall configuration attacks
feed.summary: Arctic Wolf has identified a new wave of automated attacks targeting Fortinet FortiGate devices since January 15, 2026. These attacks involve unauthorized firewall configuration changes, including creating generic persistence accounts, enabling VPN access, and exfiltrating firewall configurations. This activity mirrors a December 2025 campaign that exploited critical Fortinet authentication bypass flaws (CVE-2025-59718 and CVE-2025-59719) for malicious SSO logins and configuration theft.

feed.title: FDA clearances and approvals: Boston Scientific, BD, Elekta
feed.summary: The article reports on recent FDA clearances and approvals granted to medical device companies Boston Scientific, BD, and Elekta. It highlights these regulatory milestones as significant developments for the respective companies within the medical technology sector.

feed.title: The Next Frontier of Runtime Assembly Attacks: Leveraging LLMs to Generate Phishing JavaScript in Real Time
feed.summary: This article identifies a new frontier in web attacks where attackers use Large Language Models (LLMs) to dynamically generate and execute polymorphic phishing JavaScript in real time within a victim's browser. This AI-augmented runtime assembly technique is highly evasive, as the malicious code is delivered from trusted LLM domains and changes with each visit, making traditional network analysis difficult. The most effective defense involves runtime behavioral analysis within the browser, along with restricting unsanctioned LLM use and enhancing LLM safety guardrails.

feed.title: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA Decreases Stake in Microchip Technology Incorporated $MCHP
feed.summary: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. scaled back its holdings in Microchip Technology by 10.5% in Q3, selling 73,736 shares, yet still owns a significant stake valued at approximately $40.3 million. Despite this institutional trimming and a CFO selling some shares, analyst sentiment remains largely positive with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and an average target price of $79.67. Microchip Technology recently beat earnings and revenue estimates and offers a 2.4% annualized dividend yield, while trading near its 52-week high.

feed.title: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA Trims Stake in Terreno Realty Corporation $TRNO
feed.summary: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. has reduced its stake in Terreno Realty Corporation by 12.9%, selling over 93,000 shares but still owning 634,931 shares valued at $36.03 million. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" rating for TRNO with an average price target of $66, and the company recently surpassed EPS estimates and offers a 3.3% dividend yield.

feed.title: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA Sells 14,994 Shares of Selective Insurance Group, Inc. $SIGI
feed.summary: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA reduced its stake in Selective Insurance Group Inc. (NASDAQ: SIGI) by 2.8%, selling 14,994 shares, but still holds 0.86% ownership valued at $41.9 million. Institutional ownership of SIGI remains high at 82.88%, with several funds making position adjustments in Q3. Analysts currently rate SIGI as a "Hold" with an average target price of $81.60.

feed.title: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA Buys 55,288 Shares of Silgan Holdings Inc. $SLGN
feed.summary: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. increased its holdings in Silgan Holdings Inc. by 7.2%, acquiring an additional 55,288 shares. This brings their total ownership to 825,057 shares, valued at $35.49 million. Several Wall Street analysts have issued "Moderate Buy" ratings for Silgan, with an average price target of $54, and the company recently reported strong quarterly earnings and declared a $0.20 quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA Trims Stock Holdings in The Descartes Systems Group Inc. $DSGX
feed.summary: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. reduced its stake in The Descartes Systems Group Inc. by 35.4% in the third quarter of 2025, selling 219,375 shares and retaining 399,981 shares valued at $37.69 million. Despite this trimming, analyst sentiment remains largely positive, with a consensus "Buy" rating and an average price target of $116.20. The Descartes Systems Group exceeded Q3 earnings and revenue expectations but is currently trading near its 52-week low.

feed.title: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA Invests $40.36 Million in WEC Energy Group, Inc. $WEC
feed.summary: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA has acquired a new stake of 352,234 shares, valued at $40.36 million, in WEC Energy Group, representing about 0.11% of the company. WEC Energy Group recently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.9525, leading to an annualized dividend of $3.81 and a yield of approximately 3.5%. Analysts currently hold a "Hold" rating for WEC with a consensus price target of $120.29, and the company has provided FY2025 EPS guidance between $5.17 and $5.27.

feed.title: Teradyne, Inc. $TER Shares Bought by Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA
feed.summary: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA significantly increased its stake in Teradyne, Inc. (NASDAQ:TER) by 100.4% in the third quarter, now owning 315,828 shares valued at $43.47 million. Teradyne exceeded Q3 earnings and revenue expectations, reporting an EPS of $0.85 against an estimated $0.78 and revenue of $769.21 million. The stock is trading near its one-year high with a market cap of $36.32 billion, and analysts have a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $200.44.

feed.title: Maryland State Retirement & Pension System Reduces Stock Holdings in Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals, Inc. $ARWR
feed.summary: Maryland State Retirement & Pension System significantly reduced its stake in Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals by 46.3% in the third quarter, selling over 17,000 shares. This institutional move comes amidst recent insider selling and mixed analyst ratings, though the overall sentiment remains a "Moderate Buy." The stock is currently trading near its 52-week high, reflecting some positive momentum despite the institutional and insider divestment.

feed.title: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA Purchases 372,177 Shares of Smurfit Westrock PLC $SW
feed.summary: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA significantly increased its stake in Smurfit Westrock PLC (NYSE:SW) by 42.9% in Q3, acquiring an additional 372,177 shares to hold a total of 1,239,822 shares valued at approximately $52.8 million. The company has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $51.60 and pays a quarterly dividend of $0.4308, offering a 4.1% yield.
